title: Accessible Reader UI, Untrusted Markup, Safe Links, and Dynamic Status · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent university archive receives a different publisher mix, unknown XML shape, validator state, tenant, async schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers accessible reader ui, untrusted markup, safe links, and dynamic status into a changed content-safe inclusive research reader. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of content-safe inclusive research reader with automated and judgment evidence. Learners prog…
objectives: Treat every title, person, summary, content, image, category, language, date, and URL as untrusted even after XML validation.
Prefer text, isolate a versioned allowlist sanitizer for justified HTML, rewrite links, block active content, and test changed policies.
Use logical headings, landmarks, lists, source and date metadata, language, descriptive links, content warnings, and provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Untrusted Publisher Values
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 2399 during a hostile XML and unknown-value admission drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove untrusted content safety, semantic reading, keyboard focus, status, reflow, media, and motion; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node, network, database, and host effects; then change one tenant, cursor, reader command, authorization, validator, sanitizer, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat every title, person, summary, content, image, category, language, date, and URL as untrusted even after XML validation.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Untrusted Publisher Values.
